text:
“Prohibited substance” means any of the following substances if the person who uses the substance has not been issued a valid prescription to use the substance and the substance is classified in schedule I or II pursuant to NRS 453.166 or 453.176 when it is used:

(1) Amphetamine.

(2) Cocaine or cocaine metabolite.

(3) Heroin or heroin metabolite (morphine or 6-monoacetyl morphine).

(4) Lysergic acid diethylamide.

(5) Marijuana or marijuana metabolite.

(6) Methamphetamine.

(7) Phencyclidine.
markdown:
## Title 43
### Chapter 484C
##### § 484C.080 — “Prohibited substance” defined.

“Prohibited substance” means any of the following substances if the person who uses the substance has not been issued a valid prescription to use the substance and the substance is classified in schedule I or II pursuant to NRS 453.166 or 453.176 when it is used:

(1) Amphetamine.

(2) Cocaine or cocaine metabolite.

(3) Heroin or heroin metabolite (morphine or 6-monoacetyl morphine).

(4) Lysergic acid diethylamide.

(5) Marijuana or marijuana metabolite.

(6) Methamphetamine.

(7) Phencyclidine.

subsections.text: “Prohibited substance” means any of the following substances if the person who uses the substance has not been issued a valid prescription to use the substance and the substance is classified in schedule I or II pursuant to NRS 453.166 or 453.176 when it is used:
Heroin or heroin metabolite (morphine or 6-monoacetyl morphine).
